description: Communication Style Inventory (CSI) items. 10 items per dimension, 6 dimensions = 60 items total. reverse=true means the raw score (1-5) must be reversed: reversed = 6 - raw.

Expressiveness.description: Degree to which a person openly and directly expresses themselves, shares opinions freely, and communicates in an animated, enthusiastic manner.

items.text: I am very open and direct in expressing myself.
I use a lot of expressive language when I communicate.
I tend to speak in an animated and enthusiastic way.
People describe me as very expressive in how I communicate.
I share my opinions freely in conversation.
I am comfortable talking about my personal experiences.
I tend to be reserved and quiet in social situations.
I speak up and make myself heard when I have something to say.
I am good at expressing my ideas clearly and vividly.
I rarely volunteer information about myself in conversation.

Preciseness.description: Degree to which a person is accurate, thorough, organized, and careful in their use of language and presentation of information.

items.text: I am very precise in my use of language when communicating.
I pay careful attention to how I word my messages and statements.
I tend to be thorough and detailed when explaining things to others.
I often check to make sure I have been understood correctly.
I prefer to be organized and systematic in how I communicate.
I am careful to use exactly the right words to convey my intended meaning.
I tend to ramble and go off-topic when I speak.
I take time to structure my thoughts carefully before speaking.
I am methodical and orderly in how I present information.
I like to be clear and well-structured when communicating.

Verbal Aggressiveness.description: Degree to which a person is forceful, confrontational, blunt, or argumentative in communication. Note: high Agreeableness correlates with LOW verbal aggressiveness.

items.text: I often challenge and contradict others' opinions in conversation.
I can be blunt and forceful in my communication.
People have told me that I come across as argumentative.
I find it easy to contradict or correct what other people say.
I tend to be forceful and persistent when defending my views.
I can be verbally aggressive when someone strongly disagrees with me.
I make a conscious effort to avoid conflict and confrontation in conversations.
I rarely raise my voice or use harsh language in disagreements.
I can be harsh and direct in my criticism of others.
I am willing to be confrontational and aggressive to get my point across.

Questioningness.description: Degree to which a person tends to question ideas, probe for information, challenge assumptions, and seek deeper understanding in communication.

items.text: I frequently ask probing questions to get more information in conversation.
I like to question ideas and claims that I encounter.
I often seek deeper understanding by asking follow-up questions.
I enjoy exploring different perspectives on an issue through questioning.
I tend to question underlying assumptions when communicating.
I ask many 'why' questions to understand the reasons behind things.
I tend to accept information at face value without asking further questions.
I am curious about the reasoning behind people's statements and opinions.
I often challenge the underlying logic or assumptions of ideas I encounter.
I rarely ask for more information or elaboration when conversing.

Emotionality.description: Degree to which a person expresses emotions openly in communication, responds with empathy, and allows feelings to shape their communicative behavior.

items.text: I communicate my emotional reactions openly when talking with others.
I often express how I am feeling emotionally during conversations.
Emotions play a big role in how I communicate with others.
I am comfortable discussing emotional topics with others.
I respond to others' emotions with empathy and understanding.
I find it easy to express my feelings in words.
I tend to keep my emotions to myself and avoid showing them.
I am sensitive and responsive to others' emotional states.
I can be moved to tears or strong emotion by stories or situations.
I prefer to keep communication focused on facts rather than feelings.

Impression Manipulativeness.description: Degree to which a person strategically manages how others perceive them, uses flattery, adapts communication style to create favorable impressions. Note: high Honesty-Humility correlates with LOW impression manipulativeness.

items.text: I carefully manage and control how others perceive me.
I tend to present myself differently depending on who I am talking to.
I am skilled at creating favorable impressions of myself.
I sometimes tell people what they want to hear rather than the truth.
I use communication strategically to influence others' opinions of me.
I adjust my communication style specifically to make others like me.
I am straightforward and honest and do not try to manage my image.
I know exactly how to come across well in different social situations.
I use flattery or compliments when it benefits me.
I rarely try to influence or manage others' perceptions of me.
